html:
A Dragon Fruit is a rare Fruit that has a 5% chance to be grown from Farm plots by planting Seeds (or 4.75% Reign of Giants or 3.7% Shipwrecked). Dragon Fruits can also be produced by feeding a caged Bird a Dragon Fruit to receive 1-2 Dragon Fruit Seeds and a 50% chance to get a generic seed.

Dragon Fruit, along with 3 twigs, is recommended to be cooked into Dragonpie because Dragonpie restores a lot more hunger and health. Furthermore, Dragonpie is fresh longer than the time it takes Dragon Fruit to rot; Dragon Fruit should be cooked into Dragonpie the earliest possible.

Dragon Fruit is among the better crops for farming due to Dragonpie.

Prepared Dragon Fruit

Prepared Dragon Fruit is made by Cooking Dragon Fruit on a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star. It restores significantly more Health and slightly more Hunger than its raw counterpart. It can also be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes. Roasting the rotting Dragon Fruit is a good way to prepare fresher Dragonpie.

Downloadable Content

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Dragon Fruits can be obtained by spending Dubloons at the Slot Machine and rolling three carrots.

In the Hamlet DLC, Dragon Fruits are easier to obtain, since their seeds can be bought for 10 Oincs at Miss Sow's Floral Arrangements.

Usage

Gallery

  • Original HD Dragon Fruit icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Dragon Fruit Cooked icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

"dragonfruit"
“What a weird fruit.”
–Wilson, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“It looks sort of like a fire!”
–Willow, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Is funny-looking fruit.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“This isn't shaped like a dragon at all.”
–Wendy, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“IT HAS HEALING PROPERTIES”
–WX-78, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Hylocereus undatus, or Pitahaya blanca.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“I've never seen one of those before.”
–Woodie, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Exotic and delicious.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Quite colorful, but not good for my digestion.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“It's very fancy.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“It looks suspect.”
–Webber, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Decadent, and decorative!”
–Walani, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“So exotic!”
–Warly, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“'Tis full o'awe!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“'TIS FRUIT FULL O' DRAGON?”
–Wilba, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Pretty fruit”
–Wormwood, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Bet this would look even better flying out of the Pew-matic.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Snooty fruit.”
–Winona, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Plucked fresh from the dragon. Hyuyu!”
–Wortox, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Pretty fruit, florp.”
–Wurt, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Looks tasty.”
–Walter, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
“Not even close to the weirdest thing I've eaten around here.”
–Wanda, when examining a Dragon Fruit.
A Dragon Fruit is a rare Fruit that has a 5% chance to be grown from Farm plots by planting Seeds (or 4.75% Reign of Giants or 3.7% Shipwrecked). Dragon Fruits can also be produced by feeding a caged Bird a Dragon Fruit to receive 1-2 Dragon Fruit Seeds and a 50% chance to get a generic seed.
Dragon Fruit, along with 3 twigs, is recommended to be cooked into Dragonpie because Dragonpie restores a lot more hunger and health. Furthermore, Dragonpie is fresh longer than the time it takes Dragon Fruit to rot; Dragon Fruit should be cooked into Dragonpie the earliest possible.
Dragon Fruit is among the better crops for farming due to Dragonpie.

"dragonfruit_cooked"
“The fruit's still weird.”
–Wilson, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“It looks more like a fruit that's been in a fire now.”
–Willow, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Is cooked but still look funny.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Still not a dragon. At least it's dead.”
–Wendy, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“I SHOULD EAT THIS SOON”
–WX-78, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Pleasantly prepared Pitahaya blanca.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“It tastes like maple syrup.”
–Woodie, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“A little more refined, at least.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Ah! Food I can consume!”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Grilled fancy fruit.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“It looks tastier now.”
–Webber, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“It tastes pretty.”
–Walani, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Could use a spread of pudding and chia seeds...”
–Warly, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Ye'll make a fine meal.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“WILBA EATETH WARM GOO FRUITS”
–Wilba, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Mmm...”
–Wormwood, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“I cooked all the interesting bits out of it.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Cooked the snoot right out of it.”
–Winona, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“It's been cooked in dragonfire.”
–Wortox, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“Taste like medicine.”
–Wurt, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“It makes sense that Dragon Fruit tastes better with fire.”
–Walter, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
“I always forget it looks like that on the inside.”
–Wanda, when examining a Prepared Dragon Fruit.
Prepared Dragon Fruit is made by Cooking Dragon Fruit on a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star. It restores significantly more Health and slightly more Hunger than its raw counterpart. It can also be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes. Roasting the rotting Dragon Fruit is a good way to prepare fresher Dragonpie.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Thanh Long là một loại Trái Cây hiếm có 5% cơ hội được trồng từ các Ô Nông trại bằng cách gieo Hạt giống (hoặc 4,75% Reign of Giants hoặc 3,7% Shipwrecked). Thanh Long cũng có thể được tạo ra bằng cách cho Chim ăn một quả Thanh Long trong lồng để nhận 1-2 Hạt giống Thanh Long và 50% cơ hội nhận được hạt giống chung.

A '''Dragon Fruit''' is a rare [[Fruit]] that has a 5% chance to be grown from [[Farm]] plots by planting [[Seeds]] (or 4.75% ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' or 3.7% ''[[Shipwrecked]]''). Dragon Fruits can also be produced by feeding a [[Birdcage|caged]] [[Birds|Bird]] a Dragon Fruit to receive 1-2 [[Dragon Fruit Seeds]] and a 50% chance to get a generic seed.

Dragon Fruit, along with 3 twigs, is recommended to be cooked into [[Dragonpie]] because Dragonpie restores a lot more hunger and health. Furthermore, Dragonpie is fresh longer than the time it takes Dragon Fruit to rot; Dragon Fruit should be cooked into Dragonpie the earliest possible.

Dragon Fruit is among the better crops for farming due to Dragonpie.

'''Prepared Dragon Fruit''' is made by [[Cooking]] Dragon Fruit on a [[Campfire]], [[Fire Pit]] or [[Star Caller's Staff|Star]]. It restores significantly more [[Health]] and slightly more [[Hunger]] than its raw counterpart. It can also be used as an ingredient in several [[Crock Pot]] recipes. [[Cooking|Roasting]] the rotting Dragon Fruit is a good way to prepare fresher Dragonpie.
{{Clear}}
=={{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content==
In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, Dragon Fruits can be obtained by spending [[Dubloons]] at the [[Slot Machine]] and rolling three carrots.

In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Dragon Fruits are easier to obtain, since their seeds can be bought for 10 [[Oinc]]s at [[Miss Sow's Floral Arrangements]].
